short_summary: Iran deal claim vs. reality gap
rationale: Trump's assertion of a Sunday US-Iran deal signing — contradicted by Tehran and absent from his public schedule — is the most impactful domestic-foreign policy story affecting US residents through potential oil price and geopolitical stability implications.

top_events.title: Trump says US-Iran deal to be signed Sunday; Tehran and schedule contradict him
top_events.summary: Trump's claim of an imminent Iran deal clashes with Iranian denials and his own public schedule. If real, it could lower oil prices and reduce Middle East tension; if false, it signals continued diplomatic confusion with market implications.

top_events.title: Israel attacks Beirut on same day Trump claims Iran deal signing
top_events.summary: Israeli strikes on Beirut on the supposed deal-signing day complicate US diplomatic credibility and risk drawing the US deeper into Middle East instability, with potential oil market and military involvement implications.

top_events.title: US kills Tren de Aragua gang leader in air strike
top_events.summary: Trump announced a US air strike killed the leader of Venezuela's Tren de Aragua gang — continuation of established strike pattern against foreign criminal organizations, modest domestic security signal.
